name: Rust Foundation — Community Grants
description: Grants for maintainers, contributors, and projects. Three tracks: Project Grants, Hardship Grants, and Event Grants. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis.

name: GitHub Sponsors — Rust maintainers
description: $5–20/month from a handful of people sustains most small libraries. Find maintainers you depend on and sponsor them directly.

name: OpenSSF Alpha-Omega
description: Security-focused grants for projects underpinning the internet. Rust networking and cryptography libraries have qualified previously.

name: ISRG — Memory Safety Initiative
description: Funded Rust rewrites of critical C/C++ infrastructure. Active program with past awards to curl, Apache httpd, and Linux kernel components.

name: NLnet Foundation — NGI Zero
description: European funding for internet infrastructure and privacy tools. Multiple rounds per year. Rust projects on security, protocols, and decentralisation are a good fit.

name: Cloudflare Workers Hackathon
description: Rust compiles to WASM and runs natively on Workers. Periodic competitions with prizes for projects built on edge infrastructure.

name: Rust Foundation Fellowships
description: Paid fellowships for contributors who want to work full-time on the Rust compiler, toolchain, or ecosystem.
